experiences.intentions: Key state: 4x4 2048 board with 5 empty spaces; highest tile 64 at row3,col2. No immediate merges available in most directions except moving down creates a 2+2 merge in the rightmost column and compresses tiles, while preserving several empty spaces. Goal: improve board structure and create merges without filling the board; sub-goal is to consolidate low tiles and avoid awkward top-heavy clutter. Action assessment: up produces almost no merges and packs tiles upward; left also gives no merge and crowds the left side; right gives no merge and may scatter larger tiles. Down yields an immediate merge, keeps board relatively open, and can help build a bottom-oriented structure.

Immediate opportunities: moving right merges the two 4s on the bottom row into an 8 while preserving the 64 near the right side; row 2 [16,16,8,0] also compresses to the right. Best sub-goal now is consolidate toward the right edge with a safe merge. Candidate evaluation: right gives an immediate merge and good anchoring; left gives merge but worsens high-tile placement; up/down offer less gain. Choose right.

experiences.intentions: Key state: 4x4 2048 board with 5 empty spaces. Largest tiles 128, 64 are stacked in the left column, which is good to preserve. Immediate merge opportunities: the two 2s in row 3 can merge with a left/right move; column 3 also has 2,2 vertically in rows 2-3 for an up/down merge. Goal: keep the highest tiles anchored on the left while increasing empties and setting up future merges. Action assessment: left merges row 3's 2+2 into 4 and keeps 128/64 on the left, with no disruption to the strong left-column structure. Right also merges but drags tiles away from the preferred corner. Up would merge the vertical 2s but moves the top row and risks disturbing structure more. Down shifts the big column downward, weakening the setup. Best is left.

Goal: preserve/strengthen the high-value chain on the lower-left while creating a merge and keeping space. Candidate actions: left gives almost no benefit; right pushes small tiles to the right, weakening left anchoring; up compresses upward and fills space; down creates/open spaces at the top and keeps the large 64/128 on the left side.
